dashboard.title: Saule White Paper v1.0
dashboard.subtitle: The Semantic Memory Layer (SML)
dashboard.status_desc: Refining and detailing the white paper draft
dashboard.preface_body_1: “The Saule application will be developed alongside the Saule White Paper and in accordance with its principles.”
dashboard.preface_body_2: This document does more than describe Saule. It is Saule’s intellectual, ethical, and engineering foundation: the founding source that defines why Saule exists, which human problem it seeks to address, the boundaries within which it will develop, and the principles from which it must not depart.
dashboard.preface_body_3: Saule is being developed in response to profound problems of modern life: mental clutter, decision fatigue, forgotten intentions, unfinished plans, and the loss of connection people can feel with their own lives. For that reason, Saule’s technical development cannot be reduced to adding features. Every feature must be grounded in how it touches human life, what burden it reduces, what awareness it fosters, and the responsibility with which it will be used.

v1.title: Saule White Paper v1.0
v1.purpose: A Living Memory for People, Teams, and Institutions
intro_paragraphs: > In the age of AI, the loss of memory is a silent crisis; memory must belong to its owner.
### 1. Vision: Cognitive Sovereignty as Intelligence Becomes a Commodity

The artificial intelligence revolution is rapidly commoditizing reasoning. As reasoning engine performances improve, processing costs approach zero, and switching between providers becomes trivial. In this new era, persistent competitive advantage (the moat) lies not in the models themselves, but in the personal and organizational memory that feeds them. Intelligence is general; memory is private, inimitable, and unique. Saule proposes an independent Semantic Memory Layer (SML) that rescues this intellectual capital from model monopolies and returns it to its owner.
### 2. Why Now? The Evolution of Cognitive Infrastructure
The technology sector is undergoing an evident structural transition over recent years:
* 2022-2023: Generative Era (Generation) — Mass public adoption of large language models' text and code generation abilities.
* 2024: Context Expansion (Context Windows) — Temporary window lengths exceeding million-token limits.
* 2025: Agentic Systems — Proliferation of autonomous systems taking decisions and actions.
* 2026 and Beyond: Memory Infrastructure — Standardization of persistent semantic memory layers across the industry to ensure agent stability and continuity.
* Emerging Category: Semantic Memory Layer (SML). Saule is designed as an independent memory engine and data standard at the center of this evolution.
Saule is built upon three core innovations that transcend the limitations of traditional databases and RAG systems:
* Semantic Provenance Graph (SPG): Models information not as flat text, but on an associative graph that lives alongside timestamps, intent, authorship, and application relationships (provenance).

* Super-Node Context Composition: Instead of feeding models noisy vectors, it traverses the SPG to extract relevant relational subgraphs and composes a zero-noise chronologically coherent context boundary.

* End-to-End Privacy (E2EE) & Model Agnosticism: Memory is processed local-first and synced across devices with Zero-Knowledge encryption. Standardized Semantic Memory Interface (SMI) ensures seamless transition of context capital as models evolve.

access_page.title: Saule Early Access
access_page.subtitle: The application is currently being developed with an invitation-only access model.
access_page.why_text: Saule touches sensitive areas such as personal memory, attention, decisions, relationships, and mental load. Therefore, an invitation-only access model with controlled applications will be used in the early stages instead of public registration.
access_page.process_item2: Fill out the early access form
access_page.process_item4: If approved, an invitation link will be sent
access_page.process_item5: You participate in the feedback process
access_page.expectations_item2: Saule does not offer diagnosis or treatment
access_page.expectations_item4: Honest feedback is expected from participants
access_page.expectations_item5: User data and privacy boundaries are a priority
access_page.form_reason: Why do you want to try Saule?
access_page.form_struggle: In which area do you struggle the most?
access_page.form_takeaway: Can you write a takeaway or a sentence that stuck with you from the White Paper?
access_page.form_feedback: Are you open to providing feedback?
access_page.form_consent: I understand that Saule is not clinical support

home_page.hero_desc: AI models can continuously evolve, but memory is constant. Saule is an independent Semantic Memory Layer that understands you over time, regardless of which AI assistant you use, by preserving your mental load, decisions, and intentions. In its first release, Saule is designed to run exclusively inside the Beiwe Cognitive Browser.
home_page.section_saule_desc: Saule is not divided into separate product universes. Business, creativity, relationships, decisions, and daily life are different contexts of the same person; Saule strives to understand these contexts with a single ethical and design backbone.
home_page.book_desc: Saule's philosophical, psychological, ethical, and engineering foundation is clearly defined here.
home_page.principles_desc: Saule aims not to produce instant output, but to preserve contextual integrity.
principles: Model Independence: Not locked into any LLM provider, leaving control to you.
Seamless Context: Transforms fragmented digital traces into a meaningful and holistic story.
Active Memory: Positions the human not just as a source of commands, but as the subject of memory.
Silent Assistant: Doesn't constantly await direction; learns in the background and watches for the right moment.
home_page.problem_desc: The problem for modern humans and institutions is not a lack of information. It is a fragmented memory crisis. Every platform remembers a small part of the story, but none knows the whole story.
home_page.solution_desc: Instead of building a new AI model, Saule builds an independent memory layer that preserves semantic context across individuals, teams, and communities, regardless of which AI model it draws its power from. Saule's active memory and observation capabilities function exclusively inside the Beiwe browser during the initial phase.
home_page.footer_cta: Intelligence can change. Memory does not.
